name: auth
description: Full authentication (better-auth): email/password, sessions, and — via config — OAuth and 2FA. Installs a global guard so the API is secure by default; health and /api/auth stay public.
env: # BETTER_AUTH_SECRET also derives the key that encrypts config secrets stored in the
# DB (OAuth client secrets, SMTP password). Keep it stable and secret; rotating it
# Everything below is OPTIONAL. OAuth providers, SMTP, and the server-enforced
# toggles (email verification, sign-up approval, breached-password check, self-delete) — plus the
# feature flags — are configured on the admin Settings page, stored encrypted in
# the DB and applied live (no restart). These env vars still work as a fallback/
# override (e.g. for secrets-manager injection in production); the DB takes
# Email/SMTP is provided by the mailer module (see its .env block).
# Phone-number SMS webhook (dev: SMS sink on port 8095)

inject.text:
### auth (better-auth)
- The API is **secure by default**: every route needs a session except `/health` and `/api/auth/*`. Opt a route out with `@Public()`; read the user with `@Session()`.
- Configure OAuth providers, SMTP, sign-up approval, automatic logout, and the other server toggles on the admin **Settings** page (DB-backed, applied live — not via env). Only `BETTER_AUTH_SECRET` and `BETTER_AUTH_URL` are required env vars.
- Use `npm run auth:configure -w <app>-api -- ...` and `.claude/skills/podokit-configure-auth` to apply OAuth/SMTP credentials without committing secrets. The sign-up approval gate is provider-independent; do not add provider-specific approval checks.
- Development setup: create auth tables with `npx @better-auth/cli migrate -y --config apps/api/src/auth/auth.ts`, then run TypeORM migrations. Production images can run both from compiled output with `npm run migrate:all`.

instructions: Set BETTER_AUTH_SECRET in .env (>= 32 chars). This also derives the key that encrypts config secrets stored in the DB, so keep it stable and secret.
Create the auth tables:  npx @better-auth/cli migrate -y --config apps/api/src/auth/auth.ts
Create the app tables (auth_config, app_setting):  npm run migration:run
After building a production image, run all Better Auth and TypeORM migrations:  npm run migrate:all
Sign up:  curl -c cookies.txt -XPOST localhost:5002/api/auth/sign-up/email -H 'content-type: application/json' -d '{"email":"a@example.com","password":"password123","name":"A"}'
Call a protected route with the session:  curl -b cookies.txt localhost:5002/account/me
Use npm run auth:configure -w <app>-api -- --help for redacted OAuth, SMTP, and sign-up approval automation.

Everything is protected by default; mark public routes with @Public(). Configure OAuth providers, SMTP, automatic logout, and server-enforced toggles on the admin Settings page — stored in the DB and applied live (no restart). Environment variables (GOOGLE_CLIENT_*, SMTP_*, AUTH_*) still work as an optional fallback/override.
